examples.title: FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of First-In-First-Out page replacement algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FIFO-Page-Replace
INPUT: Reference string, Number of frames
OUTPUT: Number of page faults

BEGIN
  frames ← empty queue
  faults ← 0
  
  FOR each page IN reference_string DO
    IF page NOT IN frames THEN
      faults ← faults + 1
      IF frames is full THEN
        // Remove oldest page (front of queue)
        old_page ← frames.dequeue()
      END IF
      // Add new page to queue
      frames.enqueue(page)
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  RETURN faults
END
examples.explanation: FIFO maintains pages in arrival order. When a new page is needed and all frames are full, it removes the page that has been in memory the longest. Simple to implement but doesn't consider page usage patterns.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of references
examples.spaceComplexity: O(f) where f is number of frames

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Least Recently Used page replacement using counters
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LRU-Page-Replace
INPUT: Reference string, Number of frames
OUTPUT: Number of page faults

BEGIN
  frames ← empty set
  counters ← empty map  // page → counter
  time ← 0
  faults ← 0
  
  FOR each page IN reference_string DO
    time ← time + 1
    
    IF page IN frames THEN
      // Update counter for referenced page
      counters[page] ← time
    ELSE
      faults ← faults + 1
      IF frames is full THEN
        // Find page with smallest counter (LRU)
        lru_page ← frames.min(counters)
        frames.remove(lru_page)
        counters.remove(lru_page)
      END IF
      frames.add(page)
      counters[page] ← time
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  RETURN faults
END
examples.explanation: LRU tracks when each page was last used. When replacement is needed, it removes the page that hasn't been used for the longest time. Better performance than FIFO but requires hardware support for accurate tracking.

examples.title: TLB Lookup Simulation
examples.description: Python implementation of TLB-assisted address translation
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates TLB lookup. On a TLB hit, address translation is fast. On a miss, page table is consulted and the entry is added to TLB. Real TLBs use associative memory for parallel search.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average with TLB hit, O(n) worst case for page table

examples.title: Two-Level Page Table Address Translation
examples.description: C program demonstrating two-level hierarchical paging address translation
examples.explanation: Two-level paging reduces memory overhead for large address spaces. Only needed second-level page tables are allocated. This is used in modern 32-bit and 64-bit systems with appropriate levels.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for translation with cached entries
